summary: Session traces can infer weak ENABLED causal edges, but only through a deferred, default-off reducer with auto-provenance cap fixes and manual-edge overwrite guards.

label: Proposed created_by='auto-session' bypasses current auto strength and edge-count caps because the code only checks createdBy === 'auto'
createSpecDocumentChain is code-created edge inference but defaults to manual provenance, so it is not a clean NFR-R01 auto-edge precedent
feedback_events already records enough session/query/timestamp signal for a bounded session-trace reducer
Reducer must run deferred because live inference would turn shadow-only feedback logging into immediate causal-boost ranking mutation
insertEdge upsert can overwrite manual edge provenance, so the reducer needs an explicit manual-edge guard before inserting or bumping
Noisy trace edges can pollute causal boost via neighbor injection, so source selection must stay sparse, weak, typed, and capped
